title: Thawing of feeder-free hPSCs
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1hnup","text":"This protocol describes the procedure of thawing feeder-free human pluripotent stem cells (hPSCs) using mTeSR-plus or StemFlex ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aqsd7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"30vsq","text":"General Notes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4u75q","text":"Throughout this protocol, the term hPSC is used to collectively refer to both hiPSCs and hESCs. All described procedures have been tested and work equally well for hiPSCs and hESCs.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5gtuq","text":"Until otherwise indicated, feeder-free hPSCs are routinely grown in a humidified cell culture incubator under “low” oxygen conditions. We have successfully maintained hPSCs using either 3% O2 (3% O2, 5% CO2) or 5% O2 (5% O2, 5% CO2) conditions.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9k85n","text":"We have routinely maintained feeder-free cells in either mTeSR-plus or StemFlex. However, these two mediums are not interchangeable. Pick one and stick to it.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1r2ep","text":"We have routinely maintained feeder-free hPSC cultures on VTN, Matrigel and Geltrex--coated cell culture plates without observing obvious differences.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ajic","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare one 6-well VTN/Matrigel/Geltrex-coated plate for each vial of frozen hPSCs. A detailed protocol on "Coating plates" can be found in the "Feeder-free culturing of hPSCs" collection. A link to this collection can be found in the title section of this protocol, located above
Pipette thawed cell suspension into 10 ml pre-warmed DMEM/F12.
Place vial of frozen hPSCs in 37 °C water bath with constant agitation.
Re-suspend the cells using a P1000 tip.
While cells are spinning, aspirate the VTN/Matrigel/Geltrex solution from the coated plates and add 2 ml pre-warmed Feeder-free medium + Rock inhibitor to each well.
Add 1 ml Feeder-free medium + Rock inhibitor
Aspirate most of the medium on the centrifuged hPSCs, being careful not to disturb the pellet
Spread the cells by moving the plate in left-right, then backward-forward motion.
Check the cells under the microscope to get an idea of the resulting cell density.
Dispense the cells onto the VTN/Matrigel/Geltrex-coated plate, adding 160 µl per well.

Change 2 ml pre-warmed Feeder-free medium for each well every other day. When large colonies emerge or hPSCs density reaches 50-80%, passage using Accutase or ReLeSR. It usually takes 5-7 days for the thawed cells to grow to this point. A detailed protocol on passaging using Accutase or ReLeSR can be found in the "Passaging of hPSCs" protocol, in the "Feeder-free culturing of hPSCs" collection. A link to this collection can be found in the title section of this protocol, located above
Place the plate in the low oxygen incubator

Feeder-free Medium (version A) %s %s StemFlex basal medium 450 ml StemFlex supplement 50 ml Feeder-free Medium (version B) %s %s mTeSR-plus basal medium 400 ml mTeSR-plus supplement 100 ml - Feeder-free mediums (version A & B) are not interchangeable. Pick one and stick to it. - It is possible to include 5 ml Penicillin & Streptomycin (100X) in the feeder-free medium Y-27632 (1,000X) %s %s Y-27632 5 mg DMSO 1.56 ml Feeder-free medium + Rock inhibitor %s %s Feeder-free medium 50 ml Y-27632 (1,000X) 50 ul
creator: Hanqin Li
